calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-03-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object.

There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated May 24, 2024 was admitted to probate.

Patrice L. Gibson was appointed as Executor, with full IAEA authority. Bond was not required.

Amy Harrington was appointed probate referee.

The Court set a status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration for September 14, 2027, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed at least two weeks prior to the status date, no appearance is required.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-03-2025 Continued To Aug-05-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Wade appeared via zoom for Patrice Gibson (petitioner).

Mr. Wade informed the Court of sibling willingness to sign their interest over to the sister, that one of the brothers is in a care facility in Sonoma County, and that he will work on obtaining declarations and signatures. The matter is continued to August 5, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Filings are due by July 29, 2025.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Continued To Jun-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Continued To Apr-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Continued To Jan-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David D. Wade for Patrice L. Gibson, Petitioner.

The court raised questions regarding notice and the validity of the decedent's will. Counsel acknowledged two of decedent's brother Bernard's children had not yet been notice. Counsel confirmed that the other siblings and their descendants had been noticed. The court noted concerns regarding the decedent's shaky signature on the will. Counsel explained that a witness had to assist in completing some sections due to the decedent's physical limitations.

The court instructed Mr. Wade to supplement the petition with the names of the remaining children and siblings and ensure notice is given to them. The court continued the matter to January 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 to allow time to address notice requirements.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
